title: First Greeting at a BTS Fan Meeting

context.description: You are at a BTS fan sign event in Seoul. Seated in front of RM or V. The interaction window is 3–5 seconds. You want your first words to feel genuine — not scripted, not overly formal, just real.
goal: Express appreciation in the right register
Leave a genuine impression in a short window
context.cultural_note: 해요체 is the sweet spot at fan meetings. 반말 reads as presumptuous. 습니다체 sounds like a press conference. Emotional warmth lives in the middle register.

relationship_selection.title: Who are you speaking to today?
relationship_selection.description: Select the member. Every section below adapts to match their communication style.

rm_intp.question: You want to tell RM you respect him. Which sounds most natural at this fan meeting?

options.feedback: Too formal — sounds like a speech

explanation.title: 습니다체 sounds like a press conference
explanation.description: This uses 격식체 (formal register), which creates emotional distance. At a fan meeting, this makes you sound like you're presenting a report — not connecting with a person.
details: Emotionally distant for a personal interaction

formality_analysis.why_wrong: Too official for an intimate, brief personal moment

options.feedback: Natural — warm and respectful. The right register.
Too abrupt — noun-form drop sounds like a text message

explanation.title: Noun-form drop is blunt in spoken Korean
explanation.description: '존경함' is a written/noun form. Face-to-face, it reads as flat and emotionally absent — missing the warmth a full sentence carries.
details: Noun drop is abrupt in spoken Korean

formality_analysis.why_wrong: Noun-drop omits the relational warmth of a verb ending

rm_infp.question: You want to tell V he looks amazing today. Which sounds most natural?

options.feedback: Natural — direct warmth, exactly the right register.
Too stiff — reads like a formal evaluation, not a compliment

explanation.title: 훌륭하십니다 is for institutions, not people
explanation.description: This uses clinical honorific register. It sounds like a performance review — not a fan meeting moment. V responds to genuine warmth, not formal assessment.
details: V's communication style is warm and expressive — match it

formality_analysis.why_wrong: Formal register is emotionally cold for this context

options.feedback: Understandable — but too casual for a first meeting

explanation.title: 반말 assumes a closeness you don't have yet
explanation.description: 멋있다 is casual speech for close friends. At a fan meeting, this can feel presumptuous — even if unintentional, it shifts the social dynamic.
details: 반말 implies existing intimacy or lower social rank
Can read as disrespectful without that established context
V might respond warmly, but it's a social risk

formality_analysis.why_wrong: Too casual for a first-time fan meeting interaction

rm_intp.usage: Deep admiration — emotionally sincere. 존경해요 in 해요체.
Identifying yourself — warm and direct. 팬이에요.
Opening greeting. Use 반가워요 in 해요체.

rm_infp.usage: General admiration. Use 멋있어요 in polite form.
Stronger emotional emphasis than 정말 in casual 해요체.

rm_intp.why_it_works: 진심으로 adds emotional weight without stiffness. 해요체 ending keeps it warm and conversational.

rm_intp.english: I'm a fan! I'm really glad to meet you
rm_intp.why_it_works: Short, emotionally direct, right register. Ideal for the 3–5 second window.
Forward-looking and warm. 할게요 expresses personal commitment — not stiff, not casual.

rm_infp.why_it_works: 진짜 adds genuine intensity without sounding over-the-top. 해요체 keeps the register exactly right.
Specific and genuine — tells V what his art means to you emotionally.

rm_infp.english: You look even better in person
rm_infp.why_it_works: Fan meeting classic. Acknowledges the real-person moment. Warm, natural, slightly playful.

rm_intp.speaker: YOU
rm_intp.english: Hello! I'm a fan. Really glad to meet you.

rm_intp.speaker: RM
rm_intp.english: Nice to meet you~ Did you come from far away?

rm_intp.speaker: YOU
rm_intp.korean: 네! 진심으로 응원하고 있어요. 음악이 정말 좋아요.
rm_intp.english: Yes! I sincerely support you. Your music means a lot to me.

rm_intp.speaker: RM
rm_intp.english: Thank you. Please keep supporting us.

rm_infp.speaker: YOU
rm_infp.english: Hello! You look really amazing today.

rm_infp.speaker: V
rm_infp.english: Thank you~ Are you having a good time today?

rm_infp.speaker: YOU
rm_infp.korean: 네! BTS 음악이 항상 힘이 돼요. 앞으로도 응원할게요.
rm_infp.english: Yes! BTS music always gives me strength. I'll keep supporting you.

rm_infp.speaker: V
rm_infp.korean: 저도 팬분들 덕분에 힘내요. 와줘서 고마워요.
rm_infp.english: I gain strength from fans too. Thank you for coming.

rm_intp.avoid: 습니다체 (존경합니다, 감사드립니다) — Creates presentation-like distance. Too official for a personal moment.
rm_intp.neutral: 반말 (존경해, 응원할게) — Appropriate only with real intimacy. Too casual for a first fan meeting.
rm_intp.correct: 해요체 (존경해요, 응원할게요) — Warm, conversational, respectful. Emotionally right for fan–idol interaction.
rm_intp.tone_summary: RM values sincerity and thoughtfulness. Match his calm emotional register — don't overcorrect into formality or casualness.

rm_infp.avoid: 습니다체 / 다나까체 (멋있습니다, 훌륭하십니다) — Clinical and cold. V responds to genuine emotion, not formal evaluation.
rm_infp.neutral: 반말 (멋있다, 응원할게) — V might respond warmly, but at a fan meeting it's still a social risk.
rm_infp.correct: Warm 해요체 + 진짜/정말 (진짜 멋있어요, 정말 감동받았어요) — Emotionally expressive, appropriately polite.
rm_infp.tone_summary: V is open and expressive. He responds well to specific, genuine compliments. Avoid scripted phrases — be real, be warm.

rm_intp.question: Fill in the blank — I sincerely respect you
Complete the greeting — I'm really glad to meet you

rm_infp.question: Fill in the blank — You're really amazing today
Complete — Your music always feels sincere
